label_suggestions.name: cloud-ok
label_suggestions.description: Issue can be validated, fixed, and re-verified in Codex cloud from repo state plus setup.

label_suggestions.name: local-only
label_suggestions.description: Issue depends on local-only assets, device-specific state, or context not reproducible in Codex cloud.

label_suggestions.name: needs-acceptance-proof
label_suggestions.description: Issue can be fixed in cloud but still needs a final local/browser-backed acceptance recording.

current_batches.description: First cloud wave: independent issues with low overlap and no hard local-asset blocker.
Second cloud wave: follow-up interaction and block-crush fixes after the first merge wave settles.
Cross-cutting cleanup after the focused fixes land.

issue_overrides.reason: Onboarding clarity changes are code-only and reproducible from repo state.
The requested looping backdrop and avatar work are asset-dependent until shared asset hosting exists.
The Block Crush HUD/time-pressure issue is self-contained in tracked UI code and fixtures.
HUD discoverability is a repo-contained UI/task copy problem.
Type-scale cleanup should run after the focused UI fixes to avoid churn and merge noise.
The prompt/rendering changes are cloud-fixable, but tap-to-hear and text handling still need browser-backed acceptance.
The wasted-tap fix can be developed in cloud, but final confidence depends on timed browser tracing.
This transition bug is code-local but should follow the earlier Block Crush HUD changes to reduce conflicts.
Streaming can be reworked in cloud, but the final player-visible behavior still needs browser-backed verification.
